text: The influence of active site structure and metal-metal interaction in bimetallic iron–metal–nitrogen–carbon (FeMNC) materials and their catalytic activity towards oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) in acidic solution remains poorly understood. This paper carries out the structural characterization and catalytic performances of FeSnNC and FeCoNC compared to their respective precursors FeNC and SnNC catalysts. CO cryo-chemisorption disclosed that the catalytic sites, M-Nx sites, in bimetallic systems were estimated to be about 50% in comparison to the monometallic FeNC and SnNC samples but exhibited 50–100% higher mass activity of the ORR. The increased TOF observed with the bimetallic materials suggested a higher active site activity for the ORR. Electron microscopy along with XAS evidenced the verification of coexistence of independent Fe-Nx and Sn-Nx and Co-Nx sites, without having any binuclear Fe–M–Nx sites. 57Fe Mössbauer spectra revealed an increase in the activity sites corresponding to D1 with secondary metal, compared to that corresponding to D2. Therefore, second metal acts as a structural modifier for enhancing the reactivity of iron centres in the FeNC catalyst material and hence act as a possible strategy for synthesis of PGM-free efficient catalyst material.
